channel.title: True the Vote Newsletter
channel.description: Latest newsletters from True the Vote

item.title: China, SAVE America in the Senate, and ... Teleportation, oh my!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

What extraordinary days we find ourselves in. I hope you’re well. Here are just a few of this week’s twists and turns at True the Vote — along with ways to take action. Let's jump in!

Konnech-ing the Dots

​News outlets are reporting the release of a government advisory acknowledging China’s intervention in U.S elections. True the Vote has not been referenced in the coverage, so we've had lots of folks reach out to ask whether these developments were connected to our prior research on Konnech. The answer requires context. For those who don’t know the story …

In 2021, Gregg Phillips and I identified and researched a company called Konnech and reported our very troubling findings to the FBI.

Konnech is an election software company that runs elections end-to-end — including poll worker staffing and payroll, scheduling, inventory control, password management, e-poll books, scanners, and access to voter files. All of it. Operating in some of the largest counties in the country. All being run out of China.

We worked with the FBI for the next 18 months, believing they were preparing to shut down Konnech ahead of the 2022 elections. Over time, it became clear the FBI was doing nothing about Konnech and, in fact, we had become the target.

Because we knew Konnech posed a serious national security risk, in August of 2022 Gregg and I made the decision to go public rather than allow it to be covered up.

In the six weeks that followed our presentation of Konnech data at an event in Arizona, we were sued, debanked, I was poisoned, Gregg was undergoing cancer treatments, a Grand Jury was convened against Gregg and I in the Southern District of New York seizing all our records in the process, a defamation lawsuit was filed to try to intimidate us, and we were charged with contempt of court for refusing to disclose a source, then held in solitary confinement in an ICE prison. Ultimately, we were released by Emergency Order of the 5th circuit, the judge recused himself, and the lawsuit against us was dropped.

Yup.

I wish that weren’t a story I had to tell. But it’s a good indication of just how disruptive our exposé was to the Biden Administration. They wanted us silenced. They failed. ​

We provided all the Konnech information to the FBI/Department of Justice in 2021, 2022, and 2025. If there is the will to act, we remain ready to assist. They may already have all they need. We don’t know. We do know many counties terminated their Konnech contracts, including DeKalb County GA, Lake County IL, Wayne County (Detroit) MI, Allegheny County PA and others. Hard fought, but worthy victories.

So, I don’t know whether recently published government documents pertain to Konnech or not. We were not notified. But Konnech is very real. And our position on it has not changed.

Meanwhile, in the Senate…

Attention remains focused on what is happening with the SAVE America Act, now actively being debated in the Senate. The bill is encountering the procedural reality of that chamber: advancing it will almost certainly require 60 votes to move forward.

With only 53 Republicans, that threshold is extremely unlikely to be reached.

BUT — even if the bill cannot clear the 60-vote threshold, the filibuster process matters. It compels a public debate — one where Americans can hear, in plain terms, why the Democrats oppose voter ID and citizen-only voting requirements. Let ’em talk! The only other option is for Senator Thune to raise a procedural point to reinterpret Senate rules and then a simple majority (51 votes) could overrule the filibuster requirement and pass the bill. The catch? Once done, it's permanent. And no matter how they try to carve it out, it should be assumed that the new rule will apply to all similar legislation in the future. Heretofore, this procedure has only been used to lower the cloture threshold for nominations, but never on ordinary legislation.

But wait, there's more: States Plan to Block Reform

Meanwhile, as the debate unfolds in D.C., coordinated activity at the state level is already moving forward.

The canary in the coal mine seems to be Rhode Island. Their newly introduced bill offers a clear example of how some states are acting preemptively to insulate against the federal reforms the Save America Act will introduce. The RI bill creates new categories of disadvantaged voters We should expect to see many more bills like this one — and soon.

Under the 10th Amendment, states have broad authority to manage their own elections, and we support that wholeheartedly, but, particularly in blue states, we should expect efforts to codify expansive “voter victim” classifications and construct legal frameworks that make meaningful reform increasingly difficult to implement.

This is not incidental. It is strategic. It is also exactly why we built LegiTrack.

​

LegiTrack allows you to see these bills as they are introduced, understand what they do, and engage before they become law. If you haven’t signed up yet, now is the time.

Bottom line: The federal government can guide elections in important ways, but states ultimately control them. That means engagement is required at both levels — and your home state remains your most immediate and effective line of defense.

In April, we'll begin hosting weekly online discussions. LegiTrack will be on the permanent agenda.

To find out more, go to LegiTrack.org!

​

Community — It’s a Good Thing

We’re excited to share that the TTV NOW community has officially launched.

This is a place to connect — human to human. Inside TTV NOW, you can engage with others in your state and county, explore our apps, resources, and trainings, join conversations that matter, and begin building real relationships with real people who’re committed to saving the Republic.

While election integrity remains at the core, we expect this community to grow well beyond it — including prayer groups, special interest groups, and opportunities for connection at every level. Because we need a place to find each other again.

Important Note: We built this platform with security in mind. The onboarding process is intentionally rigorous to keep bots and bad actors out. This is a humans-only community, designed to support authentic connection and trust. If you’ve been looking for a place to plug in, you found it.

What's The Takeaway?

What we’ve laid out here, from what was uncovered and endured, to what is now unfolding in the Senate, to what is already taking shape in the states — is not disconnected. It is part of a larger pattern.

A pattern of chaos and managed decline that must be seen clearly in order to be stopped.

We’re building the tools to see it. We’re building a community to respond to it. And we will continue the work — steadily, with clarity, strengthening as we grow.

We will face tests along the way. But what is tested and endures becomes unshakable.

Keep the faith. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

​

Ever onward —
​

P.S. None of this work happens on its own. The research, tools like LegiTrack, the launch of TTV NOW — it all requires real resources and sustained effort behind the scenes. If you’ve followed this far, you understand what’s at stake. We need support to continue our work. If you can donate, we would truly appreciate your help. ​

P.P.S. You may have seen recent media coverage targeting Gregg Phillips. CNN did a hit piece and we understand Rolling Stone and others are soon to follow.

Gregg’s current work with FEMA has renewed media interest, because it serves their narrative to portray him in an uncharitable light.

Normally, I wouldn’t give this more oxygen, but in this case, I think it’s worth it.

In January 2025, I hosted a podcast on Onward, with Gregg as my guest. He was in the first week of an intense protocol in his fight against metastatic bone cancer (which, if you know Gregg, you can tell). We had an unfiltered conversation about his battle, the battles we’d shared, and the ongoing odyssey it has been. During that conversation, we also discussed a series of experiences Gregg had that cannot be explained in conventional terms.

For lack of a better word, we referred to those experiences as “teleportations” — instances where Gregg was moved, inexplicably, across distances and into new locations in a matter of minutes. Live and unedited, I asked Gregg to describe what happened.

​

The podcast, along with a thirty-year-plus scrape of manufactured controversies, has now been bundled and is making the rounds of the mockingbird media.

If you're new here and don’t know me or Gregg, you may wonder what to make of it all.

Here’s what I can tell you. Beyond the out-of-context comments and recitations of conjured nonsense, there are some things in the article that were true — Gregg’s “teleportations,” or whatever you want to call them, happened. We know how it sounds. It is nonetheless true.

It’s often said that our world is in a battle between good and evil. Stop for a moment and consider what that really means. This is a spiritual battle.

And with that comes things we can’t fully explain — angels, demons, supernatural events — just as the Bible describes. We don’t claim to understand it all. But when something supernatural is experienced, especially in the midst of this kind of battle, it should be spoken of, in its time. And now is the time, because the battle is real.

“For our struggle is not against flesh and blood, but against the rulers, against the authorities, against the powers of this dark world and against the spiritual forces of evil in the heavenly realms.” — Ephesians 6:12

To see what all the attention is about, here's the full episode.

For those who want to explore further, Scripture contains many accounts of individuals being transported or moved in ways that defy ordinary explanation including in Acts 8:39–40; Ezekiel 3:12–14, 8:3, and 11:24; 2 Kings 2:11; John 6:21; Matthew 4:5, 8; 2 Corinthians 12:2–4; and 1 Thessalonians 4:17.

We are living in days of miracles. Maybe you have already experienced something supernatural in your life — if not, stay awake, you will. When you do, don’t ignore it. Tell others! What is shared does not diminish — it emboldens, and it points to the Kingdom.

In all cases, rejoice — and gird up! There’s much work to be done. Ever onward!

​

​

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: You’re Invited! First Access to TTV Now!!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }}

You Asked. We Delivered.

Hi Friend!

It’s been a while, but please know the silence hasn’t meant you haven’t been on my heart. But in the silence, there has been much accomplished. We’ve been deeply focused — wrapping up projects, launching new initiatives, strengthening the ones already underway, and prayerfully considering what comes next.

Before I get to the main event... let's talk Legitrack and IV3

Legitrack is LIVE! We launched LegiTrack and it's been quite the journey! Every state does things a little differently (hat tip to the 10th Amendment!), and none of them match the federal process. But we’re thrilled about what this first version can already do.

👉 Please check out Catherine's Legitrack announcement if you haven't already! 👈

More updates coming soon!

IV3 Update! We’re excited to share that a new version of IV3 is on the way.

Many of you have asked about what’s next, and we want to be upfront about where things stand. The cost of obtaining election data has always been substantial, but the expense of properly normalizing, verifying, and preparing that data has grown even more in recent years.

Add to that the demands of ongoing litigation, maintaining our current tools, and developing new platforms, and it became clear that we needed to focus our resources where they would make the greatest impact for you.

That said, we know 2026 is an important year to prepare for IV3’s return. Behind the scenes, we’re developing more efficient systems, refining workflows, and finalizing the details to ensure that when IV3 relaunches, it will have better and broader features, along with an innovative "self-serve" component.

Thank you for your patience and continued support as we get ready for the next chapter.

NOW .... Drumroll Please...

Before anything goes public, we wanted you to be the first to know...

We are beyond excited to announce the launch of TTV NOW, our new 501(c)(4) initiative accompanied by its very own app!

We’ve long wanted more flexibility in how we advocate and engage—and now we have it! True the Vote Now (TTV NOW) is a new 501(c)(4) organization created to expand our voice and impact. Distinct from True the Vote’s 501(c)(3), TTV Now allows us to act more specifically on often-partisan matters of policy, process, and accountability, while continuing to educate and equip engaged citizens year-round.
​
And our first initiative? The launch of the TTV NOW Community, a new digital platform and app that brings our network together like never before.

Because you are part of the backbone of this movement, we’re opening early access to the TTV NOW Community just for our volunteers.

How to Access the TTVN Community

​
​TTV Now is ready for you—on desktop, iPhone, and … sort of on Android.
​

You can also find the app on your Apple device by searching 👈👈👈 "True the Vote Now" in the App Store

​
Now for the Android twist .... We’d love to tell you to download the app on Google Play—but, Google wasn’t a fan. After multiple submissions and appeals, they declined to host the app without ever explaining why. So, that’s that.

​
No worries, though. You can still add TTV Now to your Android phone home screen so it looks and works just like a regular app. We’ve put together simple step-by-step instructions to walk you through it.

​
However you log in — desktop, Apple, or Android — you’ll have a home in the TTV Now Community, where we can track, talk, and transform — together.
​

​

In App Privacy

Protecting our volunteers is non-negotiable.

In the TTVN Community you'll notice that your State and Volunteer Spaces require contacting our team for access. More on that below, but during this soft launch we’re still implementing additional automated features. For now, this step helps ensure that those in your group contribute to productivity and strengthen the experience for everyone.

Inside TTV Now, you remain in control of how (and whether) you engage:

  • Login & access are secure and required to enter the community
  • You choose what personal information is visible
  • You decide whether to receive notifications (or none at all)
  • You control whether direct messages are allowed or turned off entirely
  • Participation is always opt-in, never required

There are no hidden settings, no forced visibility, and no pressure to engage beyond what you’re comfortable with.

TTV Now was built to respect your time, your privacy, and your peace of mind—so you can stay informed and involved on your terms.

Free vs. Membership Spaces

As part of our commitment to building a sustainable, secure platform, TTV Now includes a membership option. Volunteers always have free access to the Volunteer Space, while a full membership unlocks more tools and state-level connections.

​
After a one-month free trial, membership continues for $5 per month (or $50/year) to support ongoing development and community growth.
​

State and County Channels

Finally...a place where you can connect with others in your local area!

​

When you sign up in the app, you’ll select your state and request access to your state’s private space within the community.

For now, we’re manually approving each request to protect members’ privacy and ensure every state group includes only those who actually live there. This process will become automated soon.

On a personal note, I’m genuinely excited about this feature — local action matters, and real connection makes all the difference.

One Last Note...

This is a soft launch. Some features are still coming online, and others will continue to evolve—but we wanted you inside first. Your presence, feedback, and steady commitment are what shape this work.

No pressure. No expectations.
Just an invitation to step into what’s next—before the rest of the world sees it.

Thank you for standing in the gap.
Thank you for staying engaged.
And thank you for walking this road with us.

God bless you and God bless America!

Ever Onward!

“Commit your activities to the Lord, and your plans will be established” – Proverbs 16:3

P.S. - To our faithful volunteers who beta tested, provided feedback, remained patient as we built and developed this community, and have always been so steadfast in our mission - thank you. Truly, TTV Now would not be what it is without you.

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Unlock the Full Power of LegiTrack
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

​

Know More. Do More.

Hi Friend,

If you’re here, you’re already part of the LegiTrack community—and we’re glad you are.

​LegiTrack was built to help citizens track election-related legislation and better understand how laws move through the legislative process. But many people only scratch the surface of what the platform can do.

So today we want to walk through a few features you may not have explored yet and respond to a few FAQs we've received.

Snapshot View

Legislation moves...quickly. Since our initial v1 update, we've added hundreds more, dozens have been passed, and more are introduced everyday.

We created this snapshot view as a simple way to understand the scope of legislative activity happening across the country, and it will be different every time you visit LegiTrack:

Learn

Inside Learn, you’ll find step-by-step resources designed to help you understand:

• How bills move through the legislative process
• Where you can get involved outside of sessions
• Where citizens can engage during the process
• Foundational election law and governance concepts

Some of our Toolkits are even downloadable you can take them with you to local meetings, upload them to share, or simply print for your reference at home:

And while you're here...feel free to get the full toolkit shown above! 👇👇👇

Download the Model Legislation Toolkit!

Other Features

Get Familiar with Bill Language

​

Understanding legislative terms helps you follow how bills move through the process and where decisions are made. Knowing the language used in legislation makes it easier to read bills, track their progress, and recognize when opportunities for citizen engagement arise.

​
​

Learn the Terms

​

Recent FAQS

"Can I find the LegiTrack web app on the App Store or Google Play?"

LegiTrack is designed to work on both desktop and mobile devices, but it is currently available as a web app only. You can still add the LegiTrack icon to your device’s home screen, allowing it to open in your browser much like a traditional app.

iPhone

Watch this brief tutorial on adding the LegiTrack icon to your iPhone Home Screen.

Watch

Android

Watch this brief tutorial on adding the LegiTrack icon to your Android Home Screen.

Watch

"Why won't the system allow me to use my password?"

LegiTrack uses secure systems to protect your data and we take your privacy and security seriously—because trust matters.

Some users may be asked to update credentials during log in. This may occur if your email or password has appeared in past data breaches. If prompted, simply create a new password—or use a different email if needed.

This is a proactive step to protect your account, not an issue with LegiTrack.

If needed:​
• Create a new, unique password
• Try an alternate email
• Check breaches: https://haveibeenpwned.com/​

Need help? https://truethevote.org/contact​

"Why don't I see my state?"

Some states may not currently be in session, or they may introduce election-related legislation less frequently than others. When a new election or voting-related bill is introduced in your state, we will add it to LegiTrack.

"How can I see the full details of a bill?"

Every bill has a "Learn More ->" button. From there, you'll find the summary, most recent action, and more. At the bottom, you can click on "Bill Text" to see the current text, past drafts and companion or "sister" bills (if applicable), sponsor name(s), current status, and other related information.

"Can I search by just my state?"

Yes! Go to the "State" filter and use the dropdown to find your state or select "US" for federal bills.

A Quick Reminder

LegiTrack is a tool—but tools are only as powerful as the people using them.

Take a few minutes to explore the tracker, review the learning resources, and begin following legislation in your state.

You may be surprised by how much you can discover.

​👉 Log in and explore LegiTrack​

Ever Onward!

​

If you can, please consider donating to help us continue building tools like LegiTrack. https://truethevote.org/donate​

Help Us Stay in the Fight
​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: LegiTrack is live! | Your walkthrough and invitation to join!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

In case you missed it, LegiTrack is live! We built this web app as a bill-tracking tool to empower freedom-minded citizens to engage with and influence election integrity through the legislative process.

If you haven't checked it out yet, here's what you can expect to see:

Legislation Counter

Legislation can move fast or, like the SAVE Act, it can stagnate — either way, you need to know. Since our initial v1 update, hundreds of additional bills have been introduced — and new ones appear almost every day. This snapshot offers a quick look at how many election-related bills LegiTrack is tracking, how many legislatures are currently in session, and how many tracked bills have already passed:

Learn

The legislative process is confusing. If legislative processes feel overwhelming, the Learn module can be a great place to dive in. LegiTrack is filled with resources to help you understand the process and engage with confidence. Under Learn, you’ll find a collection of LegiTrack-specific toolkits to guide you step by step:

Bill basics:

Step-by-step guides designed to help you use LegiTrack effectively — from understanding bill movement to knowing when and how citizens can engage in the legislative process.

TTV resources you already know (and more!):

Trusted TTV materials paired with new toolkits designed to help you take action, stay engaged, and grow confident in your efforts — inside and outside session.

Foundational good-to-knows:

Essential background on federal election law, voter registration frameworks, and constitutional principles that shape how elections are governed across the states.

Bill Tracker 101

Behind the login is the full bill tracker. Filter by state, federal (US), and/or bill stage:

Hover over any bill for a brief description of its content to help you decide if you want to explore more:

For every bill, you can select Learn More:

This is where you will find full details on every Bill 👉 Title, Summary, Latest Action and History, and the Full Bill Text*:

Only the Start...

As you explore LegiTrack, remember that this is just the beginning. The platform will continue to grow as new legislation is introduced and additional tools and resources are added.

And, we're bringing back the weekly Lunch and Learns to talk LegiTrack, Elections, and Voters' Rights! Dates will be announced soon!

We encourage you to take some time to explore the tracker, review the learning resources, and familiarize yourself with how legislation moves in your state. And as always, your feedback helps make LegiTrack stronger for everyone. If you have questions, ideas, or notice something that could be improved, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Being an advocate for election integrity is not an easy path, but it is imperative that we walk it now, together, with an eye towards the future. Stay the course. We are right beside you.

More soon! Keep the faith! Never quit! Ephesians 6 FTW!

Ever Onward!

P.S. If you’re able to support our work with a donation, please consider doing so. We are building tools and apps, hosting trainings, continuing the fight in the courts, and expanding our team to meet the moment. Every contribution helps move the work forward.

​

Support the Mission

truethevote.org/donate

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: What They Said Wasn't True — Setting the Record Straight
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

I want to begin by acknowledging something we’re all feeling.

We are living through a season of constant disclosure — headline after headline, revelation after revelation. It can be hard to know what’s true, who to trust, and where to place your footing when the ground feels like it’s shifting beneath us. The uncertainty is exhausting. I feel it too.

But here is what I’ve learned, time and again, when the chips are down: everyday Americans don’t retreat — we come together. Quietly. Resolutely. Without permission. And when we do, there is very little we cannot accomplish.

That truth has carried me through the hardest moments of this work, and it is as reliable as the sun's rising. Goodness will turn up when you least expect it. Stay focused. Lamps full. It will not be easy. But, we are in a season of truth. And with that ... here's the latest:

What They Said Wasn't True

Earlier this week, we sent a slew of cease-and-desist letters out. Here's the one sent to Democracy Docket and its founder, Marc Elias.

Democracy Docket — along with the Washington Post, NPR, The Independent, and many others — published coordinated stories filled with false claims about True the Vote. They ran simultaneously, all suggesting that because I published a letter calling for DOGE to audit voter rolls, that somehow we were the "unnamed right wing" group referenced in court filings that had been involved in allegedly improper data sharing. Well, we weren't, but that didn't stop the stories from being written. The stories all echoed one another. And every single one of them got the story wrong.

That is not journalism. That is narrative enforcement.

[Background: Marc Elias is the founder of Democracy Docket. His law firm, Elias Law, represents Stacey Abrams' organization, Fair Fight, in an ongoing lawsuit against us — a case we won in federal court. They appealed to the 11th Circuit, now we are awaiting that court’s decision. Against that backdrop, Democracy Docket launched this attack that blurred facts and relied on insinuation instead of evidence.]

So why are they doing this? I believe the broader objective is to condition the public to accept that voter rolls should never be reviewed by anyone except the state itself and the swamp's partisan NGO of choice, ERIC.

Lots of datapoints to back up my theory, from lawsuits, to legislation, to legacy media messaging, all promoting the same basic idea that voter rolls shouldn’t be audited because databases aren't perfect and mistakes might be made. That is like saying we should fear the mirror because it might show a flaw. Imperfection is not a reason to avoid oversight — it is the reason oversight exists.

We will not accept this false premise. And we will not let lies about our work go unanswered. We'll keep you posted.

Kudos to Kevin: Persistence Matters

Kevin Moncla is an independent researcher who became aware of election malfeasance during 2020 and refused to look away. When others moved on, Kevin stayed. When pressure mounted, he persisted. When doors closed, he kept working.

​The 263-page report, principally authored by Kevin, was the result of relentless, disciplined pursuit of truth — supported over years of hard work by outstanding researchers and activists including Joe Rossi, Paul (@ericciaramella), Holly Kessler, Mark Davis, Jason Frazier, VoterGA, Julie Adams, Rochelle Moncla, Garland Favorito, and David Cross; written about by rock-solid citizen journalists like Wendi Mahoney; and championed by Georgia State Election Board beacons Jan Johnson and Salleigh Grubbs.

That work formed the probable cause foundation for the FBI’s Fulton County, Georgia raid. What happens next is up to the Bureau. But the truth is no longer hidden. It is emerging — slowly, perhaps — but unmistakably.

It is unfortunate to see others now taking credit for the work, even going so far as to reformat the report under different letterhead. The report linked above is the original. A magnum opus.

Kudos to them all! Excited to see what's coming next. Truth be told ... I know some of what's coming next and it's going to be a lot of fun. Buckle up.

LegiTrack Is Live!

I’m so excited to share this: LegiTrack is officially live.

LegiTrack is a tool for citizens. It’s designed to help everyday Americans track election integrity-related legislation clearly and confidently — without spin, without gatekeepers, and without requiring a law degree to understand what’s happening.

This is about more than information. It’s about agency.

FUN FACT: Right now, LegiTrack is being tested by a really fun bunch of folks that signed up to help kick tires on the app. If you want early access, deeper involvement, and first looks at the tools we’re building, you can sign up to volunteer here 👉 Sign Up!​

Setting the Record Straight

You may have seen the recent Gateway Pundit article detailing events we experienced firsthand in 2020 and 2021, now finally coming to light through Grand Jury testimony.

True the Vote
@TrueTheVote
Thank you helping get the truth out @canncon The public was told there was no probable cause to investigate our findings. We’d been cancelled across all social media and had almost no voice to fight back. Now, 5 years later, Grand jury testimony exposes what was really going
4:23 PM • Feb 5, 2026
1918
Retweets
4202
Likes
Read 113 replies
​

Basically, it's the story of how politicos and power brokers worked together to ensure our work would not be investigated in Georgia. As public pressure mounted, their focus shifted to blaming us rather than examining our evidence. We knew it was happening, we wrote about it here, but there were very few other outlets available. We were, like many, being sued, deplatformed, and terrorized into silence.

Today's landscape isn't altogether different, but it is so much better now than it was then because now millions more Americans are awake. In fact, a recent Rasmussen poll shows that the majority of Americans, regardless of political party affiliation, want to the public to be able to inspect election records, and by extension hear the full stories, see the documents, have the facts. This is a great sign! The poll might just as easily have shown that Americans don't really care — turns out, they do!

Keep pressing for truth, wherever it leads. Together, we can handle it.

Looking Ahead—and Asking for Your Help

We are growing our team. We are launching new apps. We are fighting in court. And we are scaling quickly for what lies ahead this election season.

I know resources are tight for many families right now. I don’t ask lightly. But if you’re able to donate to True the Vote it would be an enormous help to us.​

This work exists because people like you refuse to disengage, even when the noise is loud and the pressure is real.

​

Please Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​

I’ll close where I began.

In moments like these, trust doesn’t come from institutions — it comes from one another. From ordinary people choosing courage over comfort, truth over ease, and community over fear.

And ultimately, our confidence doesn’t rest in headlines or courts or outcomes still unseen. It rests in something firmer.

“He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High will abide in the shadow of the Almighty. I will say to the Lord, ‘My refuge and my fortress, my God, in whom I trust.’” — Psalm 91:1–2

Keep the faith. Stay ready. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

​

Ever onward —

​

P.S. I meant what I wrote: I believe deeply in the resolve of American patriots. History has shown what ordinary people can do when they are willing to roll up their sleeves and engage.

But I am not naïve about the darkness of these days.

That is why we are launching a new organization: True the Vote NOW. Its mission is to rally and organize citizens around election integrity — creating more capacity to coordinate, communicate, and act than True the Vote alone can provide. In the days ahead, togetherness will matter. Not just for election integrity, but beyond it. We are building that platform now. When we launch, we will let you know. And it will be soon.

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Mount Vernon, This Moment, and What Comes Next
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

​

Hi Friend —

I wanted to take a moment to share some news with you — both personal and professional.

First, a bit of personal joy. Our family recently welcomed our first grandchild, a grandson, and my heart is full in a way that’s hard to put into words! I’m a grandmother! Moments like this have a way of clarifying everything. They remind me why True the Vote’s work matters — for our children, for their children, and for the country they will one day inherit. We cannot fail.

Second, I’m excited to share that we’ve officially launched True the Vote NOW, our new C4 organization. This step gives us the ability to engage in ways we simply couldn’t before — to move faster, speak more directly, and press harder for accountability and real reform. It opens an important new channel for our work, and I’m grateful to be stepping into it with you.

That expanded capability matters now more than ever.

This week, I’m in Washington, D.C., meeting with allies and building momentum. What’s at stake is real. If liberty loses ground in Congress, hard-won progress will be rolled back almost immediately. That reality is shaping every conversation I’m having here, and it underscores just how urgent this work is as we look toward 2026.

I remember first coming to Washington as an activist in 2009. At the time, I was green — blissfully unaware of the odyssey ahead. I experience this city very differently now. There’s a weight, a darkness. You can sense it almost immediately. But the passion of history’s call is here, too. Yesterday, instead of heading to a meeting or my hotel, I went straight to Mount Vernon — to ground where real patriots walked, to a shared spirit that still lives within us.

And so, despite the darkness, I’ve come to build on something that gives me real hope: a small, serious group of people working quietly and deliberately toward real results. No theatrics. No posturing. Just focused, determined work to fix what’s broken.

That same seriousness is guiding us as we prepare for a season of significant growth at True the Vote. Our focus is simple and unapologetic: empowering citizens to secure election integrity locally. We are expanding our efforts to equip activists in their own communities with the tools they need — from registering eligible voters, to reviewing and challenging voter rolls, to being trained, mobilized, and ready to work inside the election process itself. This movement will not be sustained from Washington. It will be secured by informed, equipped citizens at the local level.

There is much work to be done, and this season feels urgent. I feel it deeply.

Thank you for coming on this journey with us. You make this work possible, and I never take that for granted.

God bless you, and God bless America. Keep the faith. Never quit.

Ever onward —

Catherine

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: A new year begins — but the work remains.
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Truth still matters. Law still matters.

And courage is still required.

We step into this year faithful, clear-eyed, and undeterred — committed to transparency, accountability, and upholding the virtue of a country started 250 years ago, with much work left to do.

Happy New Year. Ever Onward.

​​​​

item.title: Merry Christmas to You and Yours!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

​

Hi Friend —

As we pause to celebrate the joy and hope of the season, we want to take a moment to say thank you from the bottom of our hearts. Your friendship, passion, and support has been such a gift to this community and to the work we do together to uphold election integrity and empower citizens across the nation.

​

​

​

May your season be filled with peace, joy, and the blessings of togetherness. Merry Christmas and thank you for being part of the TTV family!

Ever Onward! —

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

item.title: Merry Christmas to You and Yours!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

As we pause to celebrate the joy and hope of the season, we want to take a moment to say thank you from the bottom of our hearts. Your friendship, passion, and support has been such a gift to this community and to the work we do together to uphold election integrity and empower citizens across the nation.

​

​

​

May your season be filled with peace, joy, and the blessings of togetherness. Merry Christmas and thank you for being part of the TTV family!

Ever Onward! —

​P.S. - If you can make a contribution before the end of the year, we can put every cent to immediate use in 2026. Don't forget, all donations are tax deductible! ​

{{ address }}

item.title: Linking Shields | A Word of Encouragement
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

​

Hi Friend —

I know many of us are feeling shaken by the disorienting times we’re living through — times that demand steadiness, discernment, and resolve. The last few months have been particularly heavy.

There is a great deal of darkness.

But as we head into Christmas week, I want to share something I know to be true: there is also a tremendous amount of light. I see it every day.

At True the Vote, we’ve been spending time reaching out to other groups — linking shields — as we prepare for what lies ahead in 2026. And I’ve been deeply encouraged by what we’ve found.

All across this country, there are grassroots leaders doing serious, thoughtful, often thankless work to protect liberty and strengthen their communities.

Much of it happens quietly. It doesn’t always show up on social media or in the headlines. I know that can make it feel like we’re alone. But we are not. I’ve seen it firsthand. Grassroots groups are present, active, and growing.

If you are considering supporting a grassroots organization before year's end, ours or another, I want to encourage you to engage. Donations are the difference between awareness and action, between concern and capacity.

You are an important part of this growing community. And together, what we are weaving — relationships, trust, shared resolve — will be the fabric that holds us through the unknowable years ahead.

We are not bound by uniformity, and we never have been. What holds us together — when it matters most — is a shared understanding that freedom is worth defending, and that country and countrymen are worth standing up for.

We are all here for such a time as this.

“Though one may be overpowered by another, two can withstand him. And a threefold cord is not quickly broken.”
— Ecclesiastes 4:12

Keep the faith. Never quit. Ephesians 6. Always.

​

​

​

Merry Christmas and Ever Onward! —

​P.S. - If you can make a contribution before the end of the year, we can put every cent to immediate use in 2026. Don't forget, all donations are tax deductible! ​

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

item.title: Happy Thanksgiving from True the Vote!
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

From our families to yours, wishing you a blessed Thanksgiving Day!

Ephesians 6 FTW!

Ever onward, with much love -

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Grateful for this moment — and for you
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

As we gather with family and loved ones this Thanksgiving, my heart is full of gratitude — for God’s faithfulness, for the blessings we too often overlook, and for the extraordinary True the Vote community that has chosen to stand for truth in a time when truth is under attack.

This year has reminded us that safeguarding America’s elections isn’t a seasonal task or a political project. It’s a calling. And every time the fog of uncertainty rolls in ... when trust is undermined, laws are ignored, and citizens are dismissed, this community reminds me why our work matters so much.

Because you don’t back down.

You pray.
You show up.
You give your time, your voice, your courage.
And together, we keep pushing forward.

At True the Vote, we’ve expanded our projects, elevated citizen training, strengthened legal efforts, and built tools that will equip Americans to take ownership of their elections at a scale we’ve never seen before. We are preparing for 2026, and we are preparing with purpose.

But we definitely need your help.

As you reflect this week on God’s blessings — large and small — would you prayerfully consider adding True the Vote to your year-end giving? Every gift, no matter the size, becomes fuel for the fight ahead: research, litigation, collaborations and project support, citizen engagement initiatives, technology infrastructure, and the day-to-day operations that keep this mission alive.

​If you’re able, would you make a donation today?​

Your support ensures that when the next challenge comes — and it will — we will be ready.

This Thanksgiving, know how profoundly grateful I am for you. For your belief in this mission, your faithfulness, and your willingness to stand in the gap at a time when America needs it most.

May God bless you and your family, keep you safe, and surround you with peace and joy in the days ahead.

Keep the faith. Never quit. And Happy, Happy Thanksgiving!

Ephesians 6 FTW!

Ever onward —

PS — If want to check out your state or county's response to our recent FOIA asking about preparations to support President Trump's executive order, you can see it here. And if you haven’t checked out Legitrack, our election integrity legislation tracker, give it a look and let us know what you think! We value your feedback!

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: We Dig Wells
item.description:
{{ "now" | date: "%B %d, %Y" }} | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

Last week I sent an email that sparked a wave of frustration. Hard to tell exactly what the trigger was. Maybe my comments on the recent clean sweep for Democrats in the election. Maybe the lack of prosecutions related to everything that happened in the 2020 Election. Maybe just the general frustration of the times.

In any case, I understand. I feel the strain of these days too — the sense that truth is slipping, justice is delayed, and our nation is drifting into something unrecognizable. There is anger in the air, and not without cause.

But yesterday something happened that brought me back to center, and I want to share it with you.

On Tuesday, I attended a ministry event, where I had the privilege of witnessing a pastor and father pass on the mantle of a great work to his son. In the course of his comments, the father spoke about wells, referred to time and again in Scripture — how God used them to sustain His people, mark His promises, and carry His legacy forward.

As I listened, I felt the Lord telling me, 'This is for you. This is for your nation. In this season. Pay attention.'

True the Vote has never been just talk. We are a community. We care deeply. And in days like these, letting off steam around the "proverbial water cooler" is understandable. We can handle it. We get the frustration, grief, even righteous anger. It’s good to be honest. It’s healthy to vent.

But we cannot give up. We cannot quit the work of digging. Because a people who stop digging wells — who stop preparing, building, investing, shaping — will one day look for water and find none. And that is how a people die.

Scripture says plainly: “My people perish for lack of knowledge.” (Hosea 4:6). And part of knowledge is knowing when God is calling us to pick up the shovel.

In Genesis, Isaac reopened the wells his father Abraham had dug, only to find them filled with dirt by the Philistines. “And Isaac dug again the wells of water… and he called their names after the names by which his father had called them.” (Genesis 26:18)

Every time the enemy tried to choke off the source, God’s message was the same: Dig again. And Isaac did. Again and again, until water flowed.

There’s something in that for us.

A shallow well is easy work. It collects water quickly, but without structure it collapses. Anyone can toss in dirt and muddy it up. Anyone can foul it. It’s the kind of well built by people who want water but not the work. Convenience without commitment.

But the deep wells — the lasting wells — are different. They require excavation, shaping, lining the walls with stone, sealing the gaps with mortar. Hard work. Intentional work. Work done with purpose and love.

Those stones lining the walls are the programs we build, the systems we strengthen, and the vigilance we keep.

And the mortar that holds it all together — is love. The love Paul speaks of when he writes, “Above all these virtues, put on love, which binds everything together in perfect harmony.” (Colossians 3:14)

At True the Vote, our calling has always been to dig the wells that help citizens support our elections. The kind of deep, sustaining work that keeps the whole system from running dry. To construct programs, tools, and safeguards, stone by stone, that hold water.

I know you’re angry. I know you’re tired. I know the voices calling for conflict are getting louder.

But the choice before us is as old as Scripture: War or wells.

And right now, in this season, I believe we are called to dig.

To dig deep. To dig with intention. To dig in unity, so that what we build endures.

Some will say digging isn’t enough. It's not fast enough. It's not fierce enough. But Scripture is clear: the people who dug wells were the people who endured. The people who prepared.

If one day war becomes the only option, we will know it. And we will be ready — not because we shouted the loudest, but because we built the strongest. And we built together.

So for now, I am picking up my shovel. I am choosing to build. And I’m asking you to build with me.

Wells that deliver living water and lasting freedom.

Deep digging has never been the work of the fainthearted. It belongs to those with steady hands and clear eyes. And that is you. That is us. I know it in my bones, now is the time to dig as deeply as we can.

Thank you for your faith, your grit, your hope. These too are stones. Love is the mortar.

And together, together, we will build as many wells as it takes.

And we will never quit.

May God strengthen all of us for the road ahead, and may He continue to bless our beautiful America — for our children and for their children — until all the work He’s entrusted to us is finally complete, for our good and His glory.

​

Ever onward, with lots of mortar —

​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Sunlight, Progress, and Powering What’s Next 🇺🇸
item.description:
August 10, 2025 | Read Online​

Hi Friend —

Lots of updates to share!

First, a quick word on last Tuesday’s elections: Democrats ran disciplined, well-funded operations — using the government shutdown, street protests, and chaos to drive turnout. Republicans fell short across the board.

The takeaway for all of us who care about liberty is crystal clear: 2026 will belong to those who show up. Get involved locally. Volunteer. Support candidates you believe in. Serve as an election worker or poll watcher. Leave nothing to chance.

I’m excited to share that True the Vote is gearing up in a big way. For the first time ever, we’re uniting all of our trainings, technology, and resources on one secure platform — making it easier than ever for citizens to organize, connect, and protect our elections.

More details soon, but know this: the past does not have to be prologue. The future belongs to the engaged, the steadfast, and the faithful.

Next Up — Encouraging News for Election Integrity in Georgia

Encouraging news out of Georgia: DOJ Assistant Attorney for Civil Rights Harmeet Dhillon has filed a motion to unseal key evidence in the Georgia State Election Board case — a long-overdue step toward real transparency.

As Wendi Mahoney reports in American Thinker, those sealed records may reveal what really happened in Fulton County’s 2020 vote counts — including serious chain-of-custody and tabulation irregularities that state officials have fought to keep hidden.

True the Vote has firsthand experience with the systemic obstruction of Georgia leadership. Now, with the DOJ engaged and willing to lift the veil, we look forward to the chance to share what we know and to help ensure that Georgia’s elections are lawful, auditable, and worthy of public trust.

A word to the critics — outlets like the Atlanta Journal-Constitution want to frame this as an inability to “move on” from 2020. That’s false. This isn’t about looking back; it’s about learning the truth so it doesn’t happen again.

Those who refuse to learn from history are destined to repeat it — and that's not politics, it’s common sense.

Speaking of 2020 Nightmares Not to be Repeated ... Let's Talk About China Cover-up.

The Senate Judiciary Committee has now released a report confirming the declassification of FBI documents alleging that the People’s Republic of China manufactured counterfeit U.S. driver’s licenses and shipped them into the U.S. as part of a scheme to facilitate tens of thousands of fraudulent mail-in ballots during the 2020 election.

The FBI originally issued an Intelligence Information Report (IIR) in August 2020, but then abruptly “recalled” it and suppressed any further investigation because it reportedly would have contradicted testimony by then-FBI Director Christopher Wray.

But fake IDs were only part of the story. As our True the Vote investigation into Konnech revealed, the FBI also knew that massive amounts of U.S. election data were being managed in China — by a company whose leadership pledged loyalty to the Chinese Communist Party.

This is far from over.

If you’ve followed our work, you’ll remember the “Perpetual P.S.” we used to post — a standing list of evidence we were willing to share with the Trump Administration:

  • Geospatial data showing ballot drop-box abuse
  • Non-citizen database evidence buried since 2016
  • 10,000+ Konnech documents proving CCP-linked data management
  • Banking records tied to a multi-state ballot trafficking ring
  • Full correspondence with FBI agents from 2020–2022

I said we’d keep posting that list until someone in authority finally called.

Well — we don’t have to post it anymore. 🙂 Stay tuned.

The Latest on FOIA Responses

You may remember that True the Vote’s FOIA Project filed 278 open records requests with state and county election offices across the country, seeking transparency about their compliance with Executive Order 14248.

So far, 30 offices have produced documents, 17 have issued invoices or demanded payment (many of those for “research only,” with no guarantee of records), and an astonishing 121 have not responded at all.

The results reveal what we’ve long suspected — transparency in election administration is wildly inconsistent. Some offices have cooperated fully, while others have denied access, imposed steep fees, or simply ignored the law.

Even more telling: most of the non-responsive offices are in blue-leaning states, underscoring the uneven, and often partisan, application of public-records laws that are supposed to serve every American.

We’ll keep pressing forward — people have a right to know how their elections are run.

👉 You can review all responses and documents on our website.​

Thank You, TTV Community Testers!

And before I sign off, I want to give a ginormous THANK YOU to the entire True the Vote Team and our amazing Volunteer Testers, led by the incredible Meg Denning!

You’ve been tirelessly testing every corner of our new TTV Community platform, and your hard work, insight, and honest feedback are shaping it into something truly special — an intuitive, engaging, and dynamic app built to empower citizens for the 2026 election season.

We couldn’t do this without you. Thank you for your dedication, your patience, and your belief in this mission.

Lots of good work ahead — and together, we’ll make it count. 💪🇺🇸

Keep the faith. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

​

Ever onward —

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Across the Nation, Light Is Breaking Through
item.description:
October 19, 2025

Open Records, Honest Elections

Across America, the work of election transparency is underway. True the Vote has now submitted 250 public records requests — one to every state and to the 200 largest counties — seeking a single answer:
​
​“Please describe any actions you have taken, or intend to take, to ensure compliance with President Trump’s Executive Order 14248: Preserving and Protecting the Integrity of American Elections.”​
​
The responses have been telling. Some states are cooperating. Others are resisting. Still others behave as if citizens have no right to ask — or don’t have any knowledge of the EO.

Wild. Btw, if you want to read EO 14248, you can see it here.
​
And, you can follow the latest updates and read all the state and county responses as they arrive on our Open Records: Honest Elections pageon the True the Vote website.

SCOTUS and the Voting Rights Act’s Defining Moment

This week, the Supreme Court heard arguments in Robinson v. Landry, a pivotal Louisiana redistricting case that could reshape how Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act is applied nationwide. At the heart of the case is a fundamental question: should states be compelled to draw districts based primarily on race.

Activists on the Left argue that the Voting Rights Act requires race-conscious districts to ensure minority representation. Meanwhile, those on the Right contend that such mandates violate the Constitution, warning that “separate but equal” solutions cannot deliver genuine equality.

The outcome won’t just affect Louisiana; hundreds of similar lawsuits are pending across the country, and all will be impacted by the Supreme Court’s ruling. For ongoing legal updates and background, you can consult the American Redistricting Project’s dedicated site.

In case you’re wondering, it’s our position that every eligible citizen, regardless of race or region, deserves equal treatment under the law. Disproportionate impact is real, and can be difficult to address, but grouping people by ethnicity is absolutely not the solution.

This case will set a national precedent for how representation and equality are defined. We will keep watching closely.

Tina Peters: A Call for Justice

Many of us have followed the story of Tina Peters, the former Mesa County Clerk from Colorado who raised serious concerns about election security and paid a steep personal price for doing so. Tina has been in prison since October 2024.

There’s been a positive update in her case!
​
Earlier this year, a grassroots Amicus Brief, led by citizen advocate Terpsehore Maras and signed by over 3,000 Americans from all 50 states, was filed in federal court supporting Tina’s petition for habeas corpus — a request to be released from prison while her appeal is heard. You can read and download the Amicus here.​
​
That filing, made in February 2025, is now being actively considered by the court after the State of Colorado formally objected to its admission. The brief argues that Tina was denied bail not because she posed any risk of flight or violence, but because of her speech — a punishment the Amici say violates her constitutional rights under the First, Eighth, Ninth, and Fourteenth Amendments.
​
Now the court must decide whether an American can be imprisoned for her words while awaiting justice.
​
As this case unfolds, please keep Tina in prayer — a prayer for freedom, for truth to prevail, and for courage for every citizen who stands for integrity.

Help Wanted: Dallas County, TX Hand-Count Ballot Team

We’ve been notified that Dallas County, Texas, urgently needs more volunteers for its hand-count ballot team. To qualify, you need to live in Dallas County, be registered to vote, and can count ballots for four hours on March 3, 2026.
​
Training is provided by the GOP, but all qualified voters are welcome, so it is a nonpartisan endeavor. This is your chance to make a real difference where it matters most: inside the process.
​
If you live in or near Dallas County, please consider signing up today. Contact Dallas County GOP by clicking here.

And if you hear of similar local needs for election workers in your own area, let us know at info@truethevote.org We’ll help spread the word nationwide!

Closing Thoughts

There’s a lot happening — across many fronts, in ways both seen and unseen — to strengthen and protect election integrity. From courtroom battles to county audits, from volunteers in polling places to citizens digging into public records, the movement for transparency is alive and growing.

Please stay engaged.

In the weeks ahead, we’ll be announcing new ways for you to get involved and serve, right where you are. Every action matters. Every truth told moves us closer to the country we’re meant to be.

Keep the faith. Hold the line. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

​

Ever Onward-

Help Us Keep Going

https://truethevote.org/donate

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: Stand firm. Armor on. Eyes on Michigan.
item.description:
September 9, 2025

Hi Friend -

Some battles find you.

Last week we learned that Michigan is moving forward on a new set of administrative rules that would reshape how citizens can challenge bad data on the voter rolls and how those rolls can be cancelled or corrected. If adopted as drafted, these rules risk making an already difficult process even harder — stacking the deck against citizen oversight and codifying what too often already happens in practice: keep the public in the dark and call it “clarity.”

The state may want this. The people do not.

We didn’t hear about the proposed rule ​changes early enough to weigh in at the outset, mercy knows we have plenty on our plate, but there are some fights you engage in because silence looks like consent. We are not complicit. We are not silent. We are preparing to collaborate with allied citizens and groups this week to challenge these moves in court, because that’s where unconstitutional overreach is stopped. We’ll keep you posted. This is a fight worth having.

While we press forward in Michigan, I want to encourage you to take one simple, meaningful step today: add your comments in support of America First Legal’s petition urging the U.S. Election Assistance Commission to require documentary proof of citizenship to register for federal elections. AFL is in the arena on real cases, and this is a concrete, national lever for reform. Let’s​ help them push.​

Many of you also joined us in prayer last week as decisions in Washington that affect our work moved forward. We still haven’t received any feedback. That’s fine. We may not. If DC does the right thing, praise God. If politics prevails, we keep going. Either way, your prayers matter. The greatest prayer is strength to walk faithfully in God’s plan, whatever comes.

So here’s how you can help right now:

1) Pray. For wisdom, protection, and perseverance for all who are standing watch.

2) Act. Add​ your ​comments ​to AFL’s petition in support of proof of citizenship to register to vote

3) Stand with us. Share this message. Encourage one more person to get signed up and get involved. Soon we will be introducing a new community platform that will allow us all to connect and engage like never before!

4) If you’re able, please support the work that keeps the lights on and the lawsuits moving.

And above all … keep the faith. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

Ever onward -

Catherine

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}

item.title: We Need Your Prayers
item.description:
August 29, 2025

Hi friend,

This week holds several important developments for True the Vote — moments that could shape the road ahead in meaningful ways. While I cannot share details just yet, I can tell you that we are asking for your prayers.

Over the next few days, those with the ability to bring about real change are being given a path to do so, and we are praying that doors open for truth and restoration where so much has been broken.

Your faithful support has carried us through many battles and many years of hard work. Today, we continue to stand with resolve, believing that truth will prevail. Please keep us in your thoughts and prayers this week — for wisdom, for justice, and for strength to endure.

We know that brighter days are ahead, and with your continued prayers and encouragement, we will press forward with hope. Together, we are building the foundation for lasting change, and we remain confident that the best is yet to come.

Keep the faith. Hold the line. Never quit. Ephesians 6 FTW!

Ever onward —

​

Help Us Keep Going

​https://truethevote.org/donate​

​​​​

Update your email preferences or unsubscribe here​

© 2025 True the Vote

{{ address }}
